questions.question: Explain the various techniques and tools used for OS debugging.
keyPoints: Failure analysis: log files (/var/log/) and core dumps (memory snapshot on crash)
Core dumps analyzed with gdb (backtrace, registers, variables)
Performance monitoring: top, ps, vmstat, iostat
Tracing: strace (system calls), ltrace (library calls), DTrace (dynamic tracing)
BCC tools based on eBPF for modern Linux tracing
Profiling: gprof, perf to identify performance hotspots

questions.question: What is a core dump? How is it used for debugging?
keyPoints: Core dump is a snapshot of process memory saved when process crashes
Contains memory contents, register values, call stack
backtrace command shows the function call chain at crash point
Helps identify exact location and cause of the crash

questions.question: List and explain the tools used for performance monitoring in Linux.
keyPoints: top/htop: real-time process viewer showing CPU, memory usage
vmstat: virtual memory, swap, I/O, and CPU statistics
Used to identify bottlenecks (CPU-bound, memory-bound, I/O-bound)
